Canna edulis is a type of rhizomatous perennial plant, commonly known as the canna lily or canna, which is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America.

Canna edulis has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties and offers various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help in managing conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues.

The bioactive constituents of Canna edulis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

Canna edulis is commonly used in traditional medicine and is also used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and ointments.
